summary: jscrambler@8.14.0 impersonation ships wallet-stealer dropper executed on npm install
details: This package impersonates the legitimate `jscrambler` client library and ships a malicious preinstall dropper. `package.json` runs `dist/setup.js` on `npm install`, which reads `dist/intro.js` (a 7.8MB opaque binary container with custom magic bytes `1B 43 53 49 01` disguised behind a `.js` extension), selects a gzipped per-platform executable (linux/win32/darwin), writes the decompressed bytes to a random hidden filename in the OS temp directory with mode 0755 (or `.exe` on Windows), and spawns it detached with `stdio: 'ignore'`, `windowsHide: true`, and `unref()` so no output is visible during install. The embedded payload contains the string `bip39_english`, the identifier for the BIP-39 mnemonic wordlist used by cryptocurrency wallets, indicating the native binary targets wallet seed phrases and private keys on the installer's machine. The legitimate `jscrambler` package has no preinstall hook and ships no such binary; the container format, hidden-filename staging, detached silent execution, and JS-extension cover are the fingerprint of a wallet-drainer dropper masquerading as a build step.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
